violations.text: An employee was observed touching ready to eat food celery with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

Garlic butter was observed held at 71 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, garlic butter was taken out less than one hour prior, PIC moved to walk-in cooler at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

Ham, deli meats and pasta were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At time of inspection, large pieces of chicken were observed cooling on the counter in containers greater than 4 inches deep. PIC moved to shallow pan at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of: 1. Oven panel (grease/black residue) 2. Mixer (food debris) 3. Dry Storage Shelving (black/brown debris) 4. Scoop handle (food debris) New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3 bay sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024
Observed missing floor tiles in the mop sink area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024
Ceiling tile above the fryers were observed falling with exposed installation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024
The wall above the handwash sink in the kitchen was observed with an incomplete seal and exposed wood. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024

Employees' personal belongings (phone, drinks) were observed stored in the prep area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area(prepped pizza dough refrigerator). Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Several beverage containers were observed stored on the floor in the front dining area and back storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Shredded cooked chicken made on 5/17/23 was observed held at 51-55*F degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposal was done. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of Owner, cooked shredded chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling procedures was discussed with owner. Owner will retrain employees on proper cooling procedures. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2023

Knives were stored in between wall and equipment. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2023
The door gaskets on the pizza unit is in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2023
